description_long: This is a small, single-story cabin with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in wood or painted siding, and the roof appears to be made of shingles or tiles. The front facade features a single, centered door and a small window to the right of the entrance, both with minimal trim. The side walls are plain, with no visible windows or architectural embellishments. The cabin sits on a flat, circular base, suggesting it is intended as a standalone structure. Its compact size and straightforward design evoke a utilitarian, rustic charm suitable for parks or residential neighborhoods.
description: A compact, light-colored cabin with a dark gable roof, single front door, and one small window, exuding a simple rustic style.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from an angle showing the front door and a small window on the right side of the facade.
description_view_1: A direct side view reveals the plain wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the cabin's rectangular shape and gable roof.
description_view_2: The rear angle shows the back wall, which is featureless and highlights the roof's overhang.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays another plain wall, mirroring the simplicity of the other side.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: one small rectangular window on the front facade, positioned to the right of the door, with no other visible windows
attribute_door_placement: single exterior door centered on the front facade
